abstract: = 2,350), we find that AI management is perceived as less benevolent than human management. Given that benevolence is an important antecedent of trust in leaders, this perception has a negative effect on trust in AI management, even when controlling for perceived ability and integrity. Employees prefer human management to AI management in high empathy demand contexts, where individuals seek management that can empathize and experience the emotions that they are feeling, as opposed to low empathy demand contexts. These findings deepen our understanding of trust and provide important theoretical and practical insights on the implementation and adoption of AI management. ob.tldr: 通过2350名员工样本比较AI管理与人类管理的感知善意差异。结果发现AI被视为缺乏善意，善意感知显著削弱对AI的信任，即使控制能力与诚信因素。高同理心需求情境下，员工更倾向选择人类管理。

title: How perceived lack of benevolence harms trust of artificial intelligence management.
title_zh: 感知善意缺失如何损害人工智能管理的信任
